name: TA-Lib (Technical Analysis Library)

performance.notes: C library under Python wrapper; very fast array operations

agent_readiness.notes: 150+ technical indicators via C library (OHLCV arrays). import talib. talib.SMA(close, timeperiod=14). talib.MACD(close, fastperiod=12, slowperiod=26, signalperiod=9). talib.RSI(close, timeperiod=14). talib.BBANDS(close). talib.CDLDOJI(open, high, low, close) (candlestick patterns). Requires libta-lib C library installed. NumPy arrays as input. 150+ functions: momentum, overlap studies, volatility, pattern recognition. Standard in quant finance Python.

gotchas: Requires C library (libta-lib) installation — platform-specific build issues
NaN values at start of arrays (look-back period)
Not maintained on PyPI — use conda or build from source
pandas-ta or ta package easier to install if C deps are problematic
best_when: Computing technical indicators on OHLCV data (SMA, RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands)
Backtesting signals in vectorbt or backtrader
avoid_when: When C library installation problematic (use pandas-ta instead)
summary: TA-Lib provides 150+ technical analysis indicators via Python wrapper around C library. talib.SMA(), talib.RSI(), talib.MACD(), talib.BBANDS(), talib.CDLDOJI() for 60+ candlestick patterns. NumPy array input/output. Very fast C backend. Requires libta-lib system installation. Standard for quant finance feature engineering. Use pandas-ta if C installation is problematic.
